paper_title: Impact assessment of empowering small-scale farmers through the Mag-Uma Kaupod ang Pamilya Extension (MUKaPE) Program of the DEBESMSCAT-Cawayan Campus

entities.description: Faculty researcher and author of the study.

entities.id: Dr. Emilio B. Espinosa Sr. Memorial State College of Agriculture and Technology
entities.description: Higher education institution in the Philippines.
Field focused on providing information and support to agricultural communities.
Process of enabling small-scale farmers to improve their production and livelihoods.

entities.id: Mag-Uma Kaupod ang Pamilya Extension (MUKaPE) Program
entities.description: Extension program focusing on family-based farm production and profitability.
Method used to provide an in-depth understanding of the extension program outcomes.
Sampling technique used to select 17 farmer-beneficiaries.
Data collection tool used with farmer-beneficiaries.
Research instrument tailored to the farming context.
Quantitative and qualitative data from 17 participating farmers.
The increased percentage of beneficiaries reporting high agricultural yields.
The improved percentage of farmers reporting profitable operations.
The increased minimum monthly income reported after the program.
Full participation in positive sentiment regarding agricultural activities.
